question.label: Tradeoff process
question.name: How are roadmap tradeoffs getting finalized from what you've seen so far?
question.description: Surfaces her current mental model of the decision process before moving into support.

answer: A consistent partner check-in cadence would help me validate assumptions earlier.

assessment.note: Names a missing operating rhythm — consistent partner check-ins to validate assumptions earlier — which is a deficiency-as-request and signals unclear process plus weak growth support. [THREAD-DEFERRED-WINDDOWN] consistent partner check-in cadence.

new_queue.label: Support from me
new_queue.name: Where would a clearer call from me help you most?

new_queue.label: Next support
new_queue.name: What would make the next two weeks feel steadier for you?

history.answer_excerpt: I am settling in, but I still feel like I am learning the unwritten rules in every meeting.
I want to drive alignment, but I do not want to create noise by over-communicating.
I am settling in, but I still feel like I am learning the unwritten rules in every meeting.
I can draft plans, though I am not always sure when I should decide versus escalate.
Cross-team conversations move fast and I sometimes leave unsure about who committed to what.
Being remote makes it harder to read context that others seem to pick up naturally.
I want to drive alignment, but I do not want to create noise by over-communicating.
I still need a clearer picture of how roadmap tradeoffs are finalized here.
A consistent partner check-in cadence would help me validate assumptions earlier.
